response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable senior/staff individual contributor with broad full-stack and distributed-systems experience, but the profile falls far short of the CTO role requirements across every critical dimension. There is no evidence of engineering leadership, team scaling, or executive responsibility at any point in the career history. The AI/agent exposure is shallow and recent (6 months as a contributor at re:cinq, not an architect), with no demonstrated production multi-agent systems at scale. Fintech depth is limited to a single non-infrastructure role at RIDE Capital and a direct-mail pricing role at Optilyz. There is no regulatory compliance background, no LLM infrastructure ownership, and no experience managing external delivery partners or growing engineering organizations.
strengths: Broad technical range across backend, distributed systems, and real-time high-throughput services (Dunnhumby: millions of HTTP requests/sec with low-latency DLang microservices)
Recent exposure to agentic workflows and MCP tooling at re:cinq, showing awareness of the AI-agent paradigm
Long track record of hands-on craftsmanship across multiple domains (GIS, fintech-adjacent, direct mail automation, embedded), demonstrating adaptability
concerns: Zero engineering leadership experience — no team management, no hiring authority, no architectural governance, no board-level communication at any employer
No production multi-agent AI systems: the agentic work is 6 months as a contributor, not an architect or owner, and preceded immediately by a career gap
No fintech infrastructure depth (credit systems, payment flows, regulatory compliance, real-time risk) that the role explicitly requires as a hard requirement
